Tornado damage became apparent near the intersection of 17th Street and Savannah Ave southeast of Motiva. The tornado path traveled east, peaking out near the Progressive Missionary Baptist Church where a large portion of the masonry walls collapsed. Another home near 11th Street and Orange Ave had the roof completely removed and some walls collapsed. Numerous homes sustained roof damage along the path along many trees downed or damaged. The path continued across Rose Hill Park and onto Pleasure Island. The tornado may have continued into Sabine Lake. The tornado lasted for roughly 4 minutes with peak winds estimated near 120 mph, making it an EF2.
Broadcast Media sent video of significant damage to commercial buildings and power infrastructure. Time estimated by radar.

The tornado began near the intersection of Herbert Camp Rd and Big Pasture Road, resulting in EF0-EF1 tree damage as it headed northeast. Surging winds to the south of the tornadic circulation removed the roof of an outbuilding farther east down Big Pasture Rd. The tornado continued northeast through marshy and uninhabited areas, crossing the Intracoastal waterway south of the Grand Lake Swing Bridge. To the south of LA-384, an untethered mobile home was flipped and destroyed. On the north side of the highway, an RV and a small outbuilding were overturned. The tornado continued east-northeast, causing additional tree damage as it neared and crossed Gulf Highway. The tornado caused additional tree damage as it passed through the Vincent Road and Piccou Lane area before dissipating shortly after.

The tornado touched down to the south of LA-384 near Honore Lane, damaging trees as it headed northeast toward the Intracoastal waterway. The tornado continued through generally marshy and uninhabited areas until nearing Gulf Highway to the south of b1BANK, causing additional tree damage and tearing off a metal awning from a storage building and tossing it approximately 300 feet into a neighboring yard. The tornado then crossed Gulf Highway, leaning wooden utility poles toward the east, and dissipating in a field to the southeast of the bank. Tornado Rated EF1.
Mesonet report of 49 MPH gust at Calcasieu Lake.
ASOS station KLCH Lake Charles.

An EF 2 tornado with max winds of 115 MPH touched down near Vernon road on the eastern side of McNeese State University and crossed Common, Center, Harvard and Kirkman streets before moving into the Moss Memorial Health Complex causing damage to roofing. The tornado continued across Louisiana Ave where it further intensified destroying the roofs of several homes along Elton Court while also causing widespread tree and electrical damage. The tornado weakened as it crossed Brentwood lane before dissipating just east of Walton St.
NWS Employee reports multiple manufactured sheds blown into the road.

Possible tornado on Ryan St. at Library Riot.

A tornado briefly touched down near the intersection of Ryan Street and 17th Street, causing extensive damage to the Library Riot Bar. The tornado caused partial roof loss to an outbuilding between Deaton Street and Doolan Lane before dissipating. Widespread tree damage was noted farther east, but this was caused by straight line winds. Tornado Rated EF1.

Brief tornado touched down on a property to the west of W Todd Rd, destroying a sheet metal carport, causing overhang/fascia damage to a home and tree damage. Tornado continued northeast crossing W Todd Road and an open field, reaching a property to the west of LA-27 causing additional tree damage and overturning an unanchored wooden storage barn prior to dissipating. Tornado Rated EF1.
Mesonet station HAKL1 Sabine.

The Tornado began east of LA-101 along Ardoin Cove Road, causing damage to trees and a mobile home. A metal building had part of its roof removed, which was thrown into a field. The tornado moved east-northeast, crossing Parish Road 720A and causing damage to utility poles, then crossing LA-1131 causing damage to trees and mobile homes. The tornado continued east-northeast toward LeBlanc Road causing damage to several metal outbuildings, then passed through agricultural fields before crossing Abell Road west of East Bayou Lacassine. The tornado caused additional tree damage north of Abell Road prior to dissipating west of LA-99. Tornado Rated EF1.

Several shops/sheds with damage ranging from total destruction to moderate damage in this location. Three homes with minor roof damage as well.

The tornado touched down just northeast of Bayou Courtableau, causing tree damage and minor roof damage to residential homes on Vista Drive. One carport was destroyed. The tornado continued northeast across Camp Thistlethwaite and I-49 causing additional tree damage. The tornado reached LA-182 where utility poles were leaned over before it dissipated in an open field east of LA-182 and north of Plant Rd. Tornado rated EF1.

An EF-2 tornado touched down north of Orphan Village Road in Sulphur, then tracked southeast damaging homes and businesses. On East Napoleon St. two warehouses were destroyed by the tornado. On N Cities Service HWY a gas station awning was destroyed. Portions of a strip mall were damaged across N Cities Service HWY, where the gas station awning landed. A car was also flipped in the strip mall parking lot. In the Maplewood community several houses were damaged with trees uprooted or snapped and parts of roofs blown off. The last reported damage was on Bayou Dinde Pass where several trees were snapped. Along the path trees and power lines were also downed.
damage reported on OST.

A tornado went through the east side of Sulphur. The damage will continue to be surveyed in the morning to determine rating and path length.

Business owner reports front window blown out and debris inside at Bump To Bumper Auto Parts. Nearby restaurant (El Tapatio Dos) sustained partial roof damage. Across the street near Good Hope Baptist Church same observer reports roof missing / damage on a residential home.

An EF-1 tornado touched down in Westlake, causing roof and exterior wall damage to several homes and businesses. The tornado crossed I-10 into downtown Lake Charles, where it caused additional roof damage to a business. Along the path, trees and power lines were also downed.
Numerous trees were observed having fallen in the Prien area. This includes a tree on a home, and several trees across driveways. Time estimated from radar.
NWS confirms tornado starting near the Sasol plant and moved across West Lake and into Lake Charles. Surveys are incomplete at this time and will resume at daylight tomorrow.

A tornado touched down just west of Bayou Teche causing tree damage. The tornado then tracked across southern portions of Cecilia, crossed I-10, and into Henderson damaging numerous homes, several businesses and downing many trees. Damage consisted of severe roof damage, rolled manufactured homes and RVs, destroyed outbuildings, and at least one partial wall collapse to a structure. The roof was removed from Pats Edgewater Inn. Additional tree and powerline damage was noted in these areas as the tornado reached peak intensity of EF2 120 MPH. The tornado finally pushed east into Henderson Lake/Atchafalaya Basin where it likely ended. There is some uncertainty as to how far the tornado tracked into the basin, due to lack of access. Drone footage provided did not give indication that the tornado continued into the Basin, however, satellite imagery may be needed to finalize this possibility.

Corrects previous tornado report from 1 SW Union Hill. Corrects direction traveled from northwest to northeast. Tornado began just west of Highway 462 and north of Jim Thompson rd near Westport. The tornado produced scattered tree damage before crossing Freedom road. It traveled across a forested area before crossing Doshie Road where more trees were snapped and uprooted. From there the tornado traveled Northwest crossing Mitch Johnson road and Highway 112. The tornado snapped and uprooted several trees along Hwy 112. One of these trees fell on the roof of a single family home. The tornado then continued into a forested area east of Highway 112 where it dissipated. Tornado rated EF 1 with an estimated maximum wind of 100 mph.

The tornado snapped and uprooted several trees along Hwy 112. One of these trees fell on the roof of a single family home. The tornado then continued into a forested area east of Highway 112 where it dissipated. Tornado rated EF 1 with an estimated maximum wind of 100 mph.

National Weather Service in Lake Charles, LA, specifically the equipment building and adjacent outside equipment on the north side of the property, was hit by a quickly forming land spout from 6:26 to 6:27 PM CDT. It picked up the outside equipment and slung it across the parking lot, and across the road to the airport. Minor damage to employees vehicles, as well as the two government vehicles. Remainder of buildings, including the main National Weather Service building and radar, undamaged. No injuries reported.

*** 1 INJ *** A National Weather Service storm survey revealed that an EF-2 tornado impacted areas near Jasper, Texas. The tornado touched down somewhere in the Little Walnut Run Valley and tracked about 12 miles to the northwest lifting somewhere in the Indian Creek Valley. The tornado also tracked through the west side of the city of Jasper. Numerous power poles and trees were snapped or uprooted. Numerous homes and outbuildings were heavily damaged. The tornado weakened as it crossed Highway 190 in Jasper, only to regain EF-2 strength as it crossed Houston Street in a wooded area. There was one injury associated with this tornado. The path was extended 07/17/2024 to both the southeast and northwest with the aid of high resolution satellite imagery provided by NASA MSFC, NASA CSDA and the NASA Disasters Program.
Jasper County, TX Emergency Management Director relayed to the NWS that a tornado struck the city of Jasper. A condo building took a direct hit. A tornado debris signature was evident on radar.
Emergency manager reports damage to condominiums/apartments in west Jasper. Search and rescue ongoing. Time estimated by radar.

*** 3 INJ *** Tornado touched down south of Log Cabin Road where it moved northeast and damaged a few trees. A bumper pull camper rolled over and an awning was ripped off of a house. The tornado crossed hwy 190 and directly hit a meat market where 3 people were injured. A cinder block wall fell over as roughly 25 percent of the roofing material was pulled off the building. An old barn was also damaged behind the meat market. Tornado was weakening as it crossed Stagg Avenue. Minor tree damage along with damage to a couple of power poles. Could see a few large limbs broke off in the woods just north of this point.
*** 2 INJ *** Report of a possible tornado along US 190 west of Basile. Photos indicate significant damage to a business.

The tornado touched down just west of Ben Road. It moved northeast where it destroyed a garage, a mobile home shifted over off its foundation, and part of a roof was removed from a home. An old frame home was completely destroyed. The tornado continued to move northeast and crossed Fats Rd leaving debris across the roadway. The tornado then formed convergent patterns over a sugarcane field and 705. It continued northeast and dissipated near Toups Rd.

Beginning of Brushy Creek Tornado. EF-0 Strength. Radar tornado debris signature was congruent with location of uprooted trees and damaged pines near Lookout Rd and Highway 463.

Corrects previous tornado report from 5 N Pitkin. Beginning of Brushy Creek Tornado. EF-1 Strength. Radar tornado debris signature was congruent with location of uprooted trees and damaged pines near Lookout Rd and Highway 463.
Confirmed TDS.

Tree / power line damage across Highway 465. Tornado continued to strengthen with damage to a residential home and property where radar tornado debris signature was noted. Tornado crossed Cutts Rd. Strength EF1.

The Wilda Tornado began off of Saint Clair Rd and Kevin Ln where several trees were snapped and a small shed had its metal roof torn off. A tornado debris signature was associated with this damage. The tornado then continued north-northeast along Saint Clair Rd, intermittently touching down. Damage is weak and narrow along this part of the path and is confined to minor tree damage. From there the tornado crossed Saint Clair Rd and continued northeast, eventually crossing Browns Creek Rd. At this location a metal outbuilding was completely destroyed and thrown roughly 100 yards downstream with the tornado. A table was thrown roughly 300 yards west northwest of where it was placed. Minor damage to a frame home also occurred. This is the end point of the Wilda Tornado.
Confirmed TDS.
gage still shows water out of its banks, but nearing crest.

*** 1 INJ *** Tornado continued into southwest Jefferson County from Chambers County. It snapped off power poles at French Rd before continuing northeast. An area of trees were snapped off and uprooted just south of the Big Hill Strategic Oil Reserve. Tornado continued northeast where it destroyed the top floor of the Sabine Ranch Lodge, stripped metal from a nearby industrial building, flipped a car into a side yard, and damaged the roofs of two homes. The car contained two people, one sustained a minor injury. The tornado crossed Hwys 87 and 82 where it snapped transmission poles. Finally, it moved overtop Sabine Lake. Track likely continued into western Cameron Parish. Further analysis will be needed to discern final track length.

The tornado began off of Cooper Church Road near the community of Savage Fork where several trees were damaged near Bayou Castor. The tornado then moved east-northeast where it crossed Fletcher Road. The damage path in this area is intermittent in nature, but is associated with a tornado debris signature. The tornado then made a more east-southeast turn and crossed Hazel Jeans Road where weak tree damage was found. This damage is also associated with a tornado debris signature. The tornado then continued southeast, crossing Hwy 171, and continuing onward where it crossed a dirt road and damaged more trees. Beyond the dirt road, the tornado continued off into a thick brush/forested area where it eventually lifted.
*** 1 INJ *** Report of a minor injury as a motorist was caught in the tornado crossing Jefferson County earlier. Time and location approximate.

The tornado began just east of HWY 171 where it pulled a roof and metal panels off of an outbuilding/shed type structure. Nearby, just to the southeast, the tornado caused minor roof damage to two homes, pulling several shingles off. The tornado then continued southeast, crossing Eissman Rd and snapping several pine trees. From there, it continued southeast and moved into Fort Johnson, causing minor softwood tree damage. This is the end point of the tornado. A tornado debris signature was briefly noted with this storm.
Reports of power lines and small trees down around the New Llano area.
KPOE observation site reported a wind gust of 70 knots/81 mph.

This EF1 tornado is assumed to have begun around 1/2 mile north of Fullerton Lake, in a heavily wooded, inaccessible area. The tornado then continued southeast, cross Fullerton Lake Road where it snapped around 40 to 50 trees near the entrance to the Fullerton Lake Rec Complex. From there, the tornado is assumed to have continued southeast into to more heavily wooded areas, eventually lifting before it crossed any other roads.
Severe wind gust measured by NOS site at 4:00PM.
*** 2 INJ *** Six RVs were flipped along the west bank of Sabine Lake, Pleasure Island. Two people were injured.
KAEX ASOS reported a gust of 55 knots.

A tornado briefly touched down and cross Jeff McDonald Rd in far eastern Vernon Parish. Several trees were found to have been snapped and/or uprooted in a convergence pattern along this point in the road. The tornado was likely very brief, as no other damage was found on surrounding roads.
Numerous public reports of trees down around the town of Deville.
Muliple public reports of trees down around the Turkey Creek community.
Received a report of possible tornado damage at the Cleco Plant in St. Landry.

Tornado started in the St. Landry community where it damaged and snapped some hardwood trees. It continued southeast across farm road and hwy 29. Powerlines were damaged near St. Louis. Trees were damaged along a line extending southeast to hwy 71.
Power Poles down possible tornado.
Reports received of trees down in the Echo and Poland communities.
Received reports of power lines and fences down in the Whiteville community.
Multiple reports received of trees down in the Ruby and Kolin communities.

Satellite and radar analyses indicate a tornado touched down south west of Sweet Lake. The tornado moved northeast towards Sweet Lake where a very marked and narrow corridor of damage to vegetation could be seen. This portion of the damage path coincides with maximum intensity of a radar observed tornado debris signature. Damage to vegetation continued east of Sweet Lake as the tornado crossed the Intracoastal Waterway. Damage becomes sparse between the waterway and HWY 27 where the tornado dissipated.

Power Line down.
Reports of power lines down in the vicinity of Providence and White Oak Highways near the Branch community.
Wind damage with possible tornado.
Delayed Report. A carwash sustained thunderstorm damage resulting in business closure for 7 days. Time estimated by radar.
